title: Pagliacci / Cavalleria rusticana
full_description: Infidelity, treachery and murder are the ingredients of the most famous diptych in Italian opera history: Pagliacci and Cavalleria rusticana. Director Robert Carsen presents them in reverse order, focusing on the contrast between theatre and reality — opening with Pagliacci, in which the clown Canio discovers his wife is cheating on him both on and off stage, then relocating Cavalleria rusticana's village tragedy to an opera chorus dressing room. Conductor Andrea Battistoni leads the Netherlands Philharmonic through these celebrated works of Italian verismo.
ai_summary: Robert Carsen's subversive double-bill — performed in reverse order — opens with Pagliacci's clown discovering real infidelity within his own theatrical troupe, then shifts Mascagni's Sicilian tragedy into an opera dressing room. A meta-theatrical exploration of performance versus reality, conducted by Andrea Battistoni with Dutch National Opera.
